title: Java 19 in Action - Inside Java Newscast #33
summary: Among other things, Java 19 ships with virtual threads, structured concurrency APIs, sealed types, and pattern matching in switch - all of them as previews, but still very cool! I'm using these features here to create a GitHub crawler.
content:
Among other things, Java 19 ships with virtual threads, structured concurrency APIs, sealed types, and pattern matching in switch - all of them as previews, but still very cool! I'm using these features here to create a GitHub crawler.

This episode was not scripted and so there's no written version of what I said. Looks like you gotta watch this one. 😉

Here are links to the chapters and all other sources (basically the video description):

  • Meet The Crawler And Warm Up With Records
    • ⇝ IJN #32 on string templates
    • ⇝ JEP Cafe #11 on virtual threads
  • Virtual Threads
    • Blocking HTTP Client?
    • "Interception by Project Amber!"
      • ⇝ JEP Cafe #13 on structured concurrency
  • Structured Concurrency
    • Forking Tasks With StructuredTaskScope
    • Virtual Threads + Concurrent Data Structures = 🤷🏾‍♂️
    • Records With Collections
    • Debugging Glory
  • Data-Oriented Programming
    • Type Hierarchies With Sealed Types and Records
      • ⇝ IJN #29 on data-oriented programming
    • Type Pattern Switches FTW
    • Unplanned Sidetrack Into Deconstruction Patterns
      • ⇝ IJN #26 on deconstruction patterns
    • Isn't instanceof Bad?

The repository is linked in the sidebar.

Watch the video.
